name: JBL Charge 5 Portable Bluetooth Speaker with IP67 and Powerbank

specs.Powerbank: USB-A output to charge phones and other devices while playing
specs.Charging: USB-C fast charge — 4 hours to full

whatWorks: Twenty hours of battery life is the Charge 5's defining feature — it genuinely lasts all-day outdoor use, from morning paddleboarding to an evening campfire, without needing a recharge. The Bose SoundLink Flex and Sony XB23 don't come close to this endurance.

The built-in powerbank function is a smart feature that other portable speakers overlook — the USB-A port outputs enough power to charge a phone while the speaker is playing. At a festival, beach, or camping trip, having a speaker that also keeps your phone alive is practically useful.

IP67 waterproofing plus the JBL PartyBoost multilink system means you can chain multiple JBL speakers together for bigger sound at outdoor events — up to two speakers in stereo mode or more in party mode. The ecosystem build-out is more accessible than Sonos at this price point.

Sound signature is JBL's characteristic punchy, bass-forward tuning — great for outdoor environments where you need projection and bottom-end to carry over background noise. The two passive radiators on each end add noticeable bass presence that competing single-driver speakers can't match.
worthKnowing: The Charge 5 weighs nearly a kilogram — noticeably heavier than the Bose SoundLink Flex (590g) or JBL Flip 6 (550g). The extra weight comes from the larger battery and is a real consideration for hikers and backpackers counting every gram.

JBL's bass-forward tuning is great outdoors but can sound thick or muddy indoors for acoustic music, podcasts, or voice content. The EQ in the JBL app allows adjustment but the default tuning is clearly optimized for outdoor party scenarios.
The Charge 5 doesn't float — despite being fully waterproof, it sinks. Buyers planning pool or lake use should be careful about drops into deep water, unlike the Bose SoundLink Flex which floats.

USB-C charging is fast but the speaker itself only charges devices via the USB-A port — you cannot charge the Charge 5 via USB-A. The charging direction is one-way out, which catches some buyers off guard when they try to charge it from a USB-A power bank.

commonComplaints.title: Heavy for a portable speaker…
commonComplaints.body: I hike with it and at 2 lbs it adds up on long trips. JBL should have made a lighter version for backpackers. The Flip 6 is much more packable.

commonComplaints.title: Doesn't float…
commonComplaints.body: I assumed IP67 waterproof meant it would float. Dropped it off my kayak and it sank immediately. Luckily the water was shallow.

commonComplaints.title: Bass is too heavy for home use…
commonComplaints.body: Sounds great at the beach but in my apartment the bass is overwhelming for podcasts and folk music. Even with EQ turned down it's bass-forward.

commonComplaints.title: JBL app could be better…
commonComplaints.body: The JBL Portable app is functional but less polished than Bose's app. EQ works but the UI feels dated compared to competitors.

verdict: The JBL Charge 5 is the best portable speaker for outdoor endurance — 20 hours battery, IP67 waterproofing, and a built-in phone charger make it the most practically useful speaker for all-day outdoor use. The extra weight and bass-forward tuning are trade-offs that won't suit everyone, but for buyers who want one speaker that can handle a full festival weekend without a recharge and charge their phone too, nothing else at $199 CAD competes.

realBuyers.quote: I used this all day at Osheaga — 11 hours of playback, charged my phone twice, and still had battery left when I got home. Incredible.
The powerbank feature saved me at the campsite when my phone was about to die. Charged it to 80% while the speaker kept playing.
Dropped it in the river multiple times this summer. IP67 is the real deal — still sounds perfect.
The bass from those passive radiators is insane for the size. My neighbor across the yard could hear it clearly.

metaDescription: JBL Charge 5 review for Canada: 20h battery, IP67, built-in powerbank at $199 CAD. Best outdoor endurance portable Bluetooth speaker.
introText: The JBL Charge 5 (ASIN B098NJGJZK) packs 20 hours of battery, IP67 waterproofing, and a built-in phone powerbank into a portable Bluetooth speaker available on Amazon.ca for around $199 CAD.
